meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Freelancer’s Guide to Solving Bugs
content.seo_description: Struggling to debug code? Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to identify, fix, and ship production-ready software faster than ever before.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at stack traces for hours. Use AI-driven debugging to instantly parse errors, map logical inconsistencies, and suggest optimized refactors. By pairing Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for CLI-based remediation, you can resolve complex bugs in minutes rather than days, maintaining your project momentum.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your terminal is bleeding red error messages. You’ve been staring at the same block of logic for forty minutes, and the solution feels just out of reach. This is the 'freelancer's wall'—where momentum goes to die and profitability takes a nosedive.

But what if you didn't have to debug alone? Modern AI tooling has shifted the paradigm from manual labor to architectural oversight. By integrating specialized agents into your workflow, you transform from a struggling coder into an elite system architect who resolves issues at the speed of thought.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope. Use AI to scan your logs and pinpoint the exact line causing the runtime exception.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these logs and identify the root cause of the current runtime exception. Provide a brief summary of the failure point.

steps.title: Map Logical Dependencies
steps.description: Understand how the broken component interacts with the rest of the codebase to prevent regression errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Visualize the dependency graph for this module. Where does the data flow break down?

steps.title: Generate a Remediation Strategy
steps.description: Draft a plan to fix the bug without introducing side effects. Focus on clean code principles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor strategy that fixes the logic error while maintaining performance and readability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude plan 'fix logic error in target file'

steps.title: Execute the Fix
steps.description: Apply the code changes directly. Use AI to write the patch and ensure it matches the existing project style.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply the proposed fix to the codebase, ensuring all imports remain valid.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply fix based on plan'

steps.title: Verify and Test
steps.description: Automate the verification of your fix by generating unit tests that target the specific bug you just solved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test to verify this bug is resolved and won't reappear in future builds.

pro_tips: Always require the AI to explain the 'why' behind a fix; never blindly accept a patch you don't understand.
Use Claude Code's --chat-only flag when you want to brainstorm architectural changes without modifying files directly.
Treat your error logs as 'system telemetry'—feed them into Antigravity to spot patterns across multiple microservices.
When debugging, ask the AI to suggest three alternative solutions and pick the one with the lowest technical debt.
Keep a 'debug-context' file that stores your project's specific coding standards; feed this to the agent to ensure consistency.
content.next_steps: Once the bug is squashed, commit your changes, update your documentation, and move to your next feature ticket. Consistency is the key to scaling your freelance business.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to stop debugging and start building. Stop letting bugs dictate your income and start shipping with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
